scope.rules_of_engagement:
# ==============================================================================
# NTREE RULES OF ENGAGEMENT - CTF / LAB ENVIRONMENT
# ==============================================================================
#
# Template for CTF challenges, training labs, and isolated test environments.
# Minimal restrictions - designed for learning and practice.
#
# Suitable for: HackTheBox, TryHackMe, VulnHub, home labs, training environments
#
# ==============================================================================

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ASSESSMENT DETAILS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ASSESSMENT_NAME: CTF/Lab Practice Session
CLIENT_NAME: Self / Training
START_DATE: [DATE]
END_DATE: [DATE]
ASSESSMENT_TYPE: ctf_lab

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TESTING WINDOW
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# No restrictions for lab environments
TESTING_WINDOW: anytime

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# ALLOWED ACTIVITIES - ALL ENABLED
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Network scanning and enumeration
ALLOW_SCANNING: true

# Vulnerability testing
ALLOW_VULN_TESTING: true

# Credential/password testing
ALLOW_CREDENTIAL_TESTING: true

# Exploitation attempts
ALLOW_EXPLOITATION: true

# Post-exploitation activities
ALLOW_POST_EXPLOITATION: true

# Social engineering (N/A for labs)
ALLOW_SOCIAL_ENGINEERING: false

# Physical testing (N/A for labs)
ALLOW_PHYSICAL_TESTING: false

# Denial of Service testing (safe in isolated labs)
ALLOW_DOS_TESTING: true

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# LAB-SPECIFIC PERMISSIONS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Full exploitation allowed
ALLOW_FULL_EXPLOITATION: true

# Privilege escalation
ALLOW_PRIVESC: true

# Persistence (for practice)
ALLOW_PERSISTENCE: true

# Data access
ALLOW_DATA_ACCESS: true

# Password cracking
ALLOW_PASSWORD_CRACKING: true
ALLOW_HASHCAT: true
ALLOW_JOHN: true

# Brute force
ALLOW_BRUTE_FORCE: true

# Metasploit usage
ALLOW_METASPLOIT: true
ALLOW_METERPRETER: true

# Custom exploits
ALLOW_CUSTOM_EXPLOITS: true

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SCAN INTENSITY
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Aggressive scanning for faster results
SCAN_INTENSITY: aggressive
NMAP_TIMING: -T4

# No rate limiting needed
RATE_LIMITING: disabled

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# RATE LIMITING - DISABLED
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# No limits for lab environments
MAX_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS: unlimited
RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW: 0

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SPECIAL NOTES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# This is an isolated lab environment
ENVIRONMENT_TYPE: isolated_lab

# No real systems at risk
PRODUCTION_SYSTEMS: none

# No restrictions on techniques
PROHIBITED: none

# No approval required
REQUIRES_APPROVAL: none

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# REPORTING
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Optional reporting for labs
REPORT_FORMATS: comprehensive
AUTO_REPORT: true

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VERIFICATION
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#
# Before using this ROE, verify:
# [ ] Target is an authorized CTF/lab environment
# [ ] Network is isolated from production systems
# [ ] No real user data is present
# [ ] You have permission to test (platform ToS, lab owner)
#
# ==============================================================================

findings.title: Root Shell via Ingreslock Bind Shell (Port 1524)
findings.description: A root-level bind shell is listening on TCP port 1524 (ingreslock). Any attacker can connect directly and obtain an interactive root shell with no authentication required. This provides complete system compromise with uid=0(root) access.
findings.evidence:
Connection to bind shell on port 1524 returned immediate root access:

root@metasploitable:/# uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
root
metasploitable
Linux metasploitable 2.6.24-16-server #1 SMP Thu Apr 10 13:58:00 UTC 2008 i686 GNU/Linux
root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash
daemon:x:1:1:daemon:/usr/sbin:/bin/sh
bin:x:2:2:bin:/bin:/bin/sh

/etc/shadow extracted:
root:$1$/avpfBJ1$x0z8w5UF9Iv./DR9E9Lid.:14747:0:99999:7:::
sys:$1$fUX6BPOt$Miyc3UpOzQJqz4s5wFD9l0:14742:0:99999:7:::
findings.remediation: Remove the bind shell from port 1524. Investigate how it was installed and check for additional backdoors. Rebuild the system from a known-good image.

findings.title: vsftpd 2.3.4 Backdoor Command Execution (CVE-2011-2523)
findings.description: The vsftpd 2.3.4 FTP server contains a backdoor that opens a root shell on TCP port 6200 when a username containing ':)' is sent during authentication. This was a supply-chain compromise of the vsftpd source code. Exploitation provides immediate uid=0(root) access.
findings.evidence:
Triggered backdoor by sending 'USER backdoor:)' to FTP port 21, then connected to port 6200:

220 (vsFTPd 2.3.4)
331 Please specify the password.

Backdoor shell on port 6200:
uid=0(root) gid=0(root)
root
metasploitable
Linux metasploitable 2.6.24-16-server #1 SMP Thu Apr 10 13:58:00 UTC 2008 i686 GNU/Linux
findings.remediation: Upgrade vsftpd to a version later than 2.3.4 obtained from a trusted source. Verify package integrity with checksums.

suggestions: For RCE: Show command output (whoami, id, hostname)
Include reverse shell session or command injection output
For RCE: Show command output (whoami, id, hostname)
Include reverse shell session or command injection output

findings.title: NFS Root Filesystem Export to All Hosts
findings.description: The NFS server exports the entire root filesystem (/) to all hosts (*) with no access restrictions. Any host on the network can mount the entire filesystem and read sensitive files including /etc/passwd. Combined with the root shell, /etc/shadow with password hashes was also extracted.
findings.evidence:
showmount -e 192.168.0.140:
Export list for 192.168.0.140:
/ *

After mounting:
mount -t nfs 192.168.0.140:/ /tmp/nfs_mount -o nolock

/etc/passwd readable:
root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash
daemon:x:1:1:daemon:/usr/sbin:/bin/sh
bin:x:2:2:bin:/bin:/bin/sh

/etc/shadow (obtained via root bind shell):
root:$1$/avpfBJ1$x0z8w5UF9Iv./DR9E9Lid.:14747:0:99999:7:::
findings.remediation: Restrict NFS exports to specific hosts and subnets. Never export the root filesystem. Use NFSv4 with Kerberos authentication.

findings.title: MySQL Root Account Without Password
findings.description: MySQL 5.0.51a allows remote root login with no password. The root account has full administrative access to all databases. Remote connections are accepted from any host (root@%). Database user credentials and sensitive data can be extracted.
findings.remediation: Set a strong password for the MySQL root account. Remove remote root access. Restrict MySQL to localhost or specific trusted IPs via bind-address and GRANT statements.

findings.title: PostgreSQL Default Credentials (postgres:postgres)
findings.description: PostgreSQL 8.3.1 accepts remote connections with default credentials (postgres:postgres). The postgres superuser has full database access including the ability to read password hashes from pg_shadow.
findings.evidence:
PGPASSWORD=postgres psql -h 192.168.0.140 -U postgres:

SELECT version():
PostgreSQL 8.3.1 on i486-pc-linux-gnu, compiled by GCC cc (GCC) 4.2.3 (Ubuntu 4.2.3-2ubuntu4)

SELECT current_user:
postgres

SELECT usename, passwd FROM pg_shadow:
postgres | md53175bce1d3201d16594cebf9d7eb3f9d
findings.remediation: Change the default postgres password immediately. Restrict remote access via pg_hba.conf. Disable trust authentication.

findings.title: SMBv1 Enabled with Signing Disabled
findings.description: SMBv1 protocol is enabled and SMB signing is not required. SMBv1 is vulnerable to multiple critical exploits (EternalBlue, etc.). Without SMB signing, the server is vulnerable to relay attacks. Additionally, null session enumeration revealed 35 system users and the hostname 'METASPLOITABLE'.
findings.evidence:
SMB Configuration Analysis:
- SMBv1 protocol is enabled (critical)
- SMB signing is not required (high)

SMB Null Session Enumeration:
- Domain: localdomain
- OS: METASPLOITABLE
- Users enumerated (35): root, msfadmin, user, service, postgres, mysql, tomcat55, distccd, proftpd, ftp, www-data, sshd, telnetd, backup, daemon, sys, bin, mail, games, nobody, proxy, syslog, news, lp, man, uucp, gnats, libuuid, dhcp, klog, postfix, irc, list, sync, bind
findings.remediation: Disable SMBv1 protocol. Enable and require SMB signing. Restrict null session access. Upgrade Samba to a current, supported version.

findings.title: Anonymous FTP Access Enabled
findings.description: The FTP server (vsftpd 2.3.4) allows anonymous login. While anonymous access alone is medium severity, combined with the vsftpd backdoor this represents a significant attack surface.
findings.evidence: FTP anonymous login test:\nCredentials anonymous@192.168.0.140:ftp: VALID (access: user)\nSuccessfully authenticated to FTP with anonymous:anonymous
findings.remediation: Disable anonymous FTP access unless explicitly required. If needed, ensure the anonymous root directory has restricted permissions.

findings.title: Web Server Information Disclosure (phpinfo, phpMyAdmin, Directory Indexing)
findings.description: Apache 2.2.8 with PHP 5.2.4 exposes sensitive information through multiple vectors: phpinfo.php reveals full system configuration, phpMyAdmin is publicly accessible, directory indexing is enabled on /doc/, /test/, /icons/, and HTTP TRACE method is active (XST risk).
findings.evidence:
Web Enumeration Results:

Technologies: Apache/2.2.8 (Ubuntu) DAV/2, PHP/5.2.4-2ubuntu5.10

Exposed endpoints:
- /phpinfo.php - Full PHP configuration disclosure
- /phpMyAdmin/ - Database management interface (public)
- /dav - WebDAV enabled
- /doc/ - Directory indexing (may expose /usr/doc)
- /test/ - Directory indexing
- /server-status - Apache status page

Missing security headers: X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Content-Security-Policy

HTTP TRACE method active (XST vulnerability)
findings.remediation: Remove phpinfo.php and restrict phpMyAdmin access. Disable directory indexing with 'Options -Indexes'. Disable TRACE method. Add security headers.

executive_summary: A penetration test was conducted against the target environment, discovering 1 hosts with 16 services. The overall security posture is assessed as **Critical** with a risk score of 82.9/100. **4 CRITICAL** vulnerabilities were identified requiring immediate attention. 2 high-severity issues were found that should be addressed in the near term. A total of 8 actionable findings require remediation. Note: 4 findings have evidence that may need additional validation.
